claims.claim: Anthropic received a US government export control directive on June 12, 2026 at 5:21pm ET ordering suspension of all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national
Both Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were disabled for ALL customers globally because Anthropic cannot selectively block foreign nationals within its customer base
Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are the same underlying model; Fable 5 has safety classifiers that route cybersecurity, biology/chemistry, and distillation queries to Opus 4.8
The government's stated concern was a specific jailbreak technique used to identify a small number of previously known, minor vulnerabilities
Anthropic states the disclosed capability is 'widely available from other models (including OpenAI's GPT-5.5)' and 'used every day by the defenders who keep systems safe'
No universal jailbreak was found across over 1,000 hours of internal and external red-teaming; Fable 5 complied with zero harmful single-turn cyber requests across 30 public jailbreak techniques
Fable 5 is priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens; 90% prompt caching discount applies
Mythos 5 is Anthropic's most capable model on cybersecurity tasks; Mythos Preview scored 83.1% on CyberGym vs 66.6% for Claude Opus 4.6
Project Glasswing partners found more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across systemically important software using Mythos Preview
Anthropic's Fable 5 fallback classifiers trigger in less than 5% of sessions; more than 95% of sessions involve no fallback to Opus 4.8
Anthropic argues the action sets a precedent that 'would essentially halt all new model deployments for all frontier model providers' if applied consistently
The Trump administration ordered US government agencies to stop using Anthropic models on February 27, 2026, and the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a 'supply chain risk'
A federal judge granted a temporary injunction against the Pentagon actions on March 26, 2026, writing the case 'appears to be classic First Amendment retaliation'
Anthropic voluntarily stopped selling to groups majority-owned by Chinese, Russian, Iranian, or North Korean entities in September 2025
The Bernstein v. United States district court and original Ninth Circuit panel held that encryption source code is speech protected by the First Amendment (panel opinion later vacated en banc)
The UK AISI made 'progress towards one' jailbreak within a 'brief initial testing window' against Fable 5, though no universal jailbreak was found by any tester
Mythos 5 accelerated drug design by approximately 10x; 9 of 14 protein targets yielded strong drug design candidates
Mythos 5 assembled single-cell data for millions of cells across 138 animal species and trained a custom ML model that outperformed a Science-published model while being 100x smaller
The June 2026 Trump AI Executive Order requires 30-day pre-release government access for cybersecurity review of frontier models

Anthropic stated 'perfect jailbreak resistance is not currently possible for any model provider' and that Fable 5's defense-in-depth approach makes risks 'comparable to existing models already deployed across the industry'
